heading: Snow water supply forecasting
label: 1477 Snow water supply forecasting

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Snow Water Supply Forecasting Program Authorization Act”.

children.heading: Definition of program
children.label: b Definition of program
children.text: In this section, the term “program” means the Snow Water Supply Forecasting Program established by subsection (c).

children.heading: Snow Water Supply Forecasting Program
children.label: c Snow Water Supply Forecasting Program

children.heading: Program establishment
children.label: 1 Program establishment
children.text: The Snow Water Supply Forecasting Program is hereby established within the Department of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: develop the program framework in coordination with other Federal agencies pursuant to subsection (d), culminating in the report required under subsection (d)(3); and

children.label: B
children.text: after submitting the report required by subsection (d)(3), implement activities to improve snowpack measurement in particular watersheds pursuant to subsection (e).

children.heading: Development of program framework in coordination with other Federal agencies
children.label: d Development of program framework in coordination with other Federal agencies
A
children.text: synthetic aperture radar;

children.label: B
children.text: laser altimetry; and

children.label: C
children.text: other emerging technologies that the Secretary determines are likely to provide more accurate or timely snowpack measurement data.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 2 Coordination
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretary shall coordinate data use and collection efforts with other Federal agencies that use or may benefit from the use of emerging technologies for snowpack measurement.

children.label: A
children.text: summarizes the use of emerging technologies pursuant to this section;

children.label: B
children.text: describes benefits derived from the use of technologies summarized under subparagraph (A) related to the environment and increased water supply reliability; and

children.label: C
children.text: describes how Federal agencies will coordinate to implement emerging technologies.

children.heading: Activities implementing framework
children.label: 1 Activities implementing framework
children.text: After submitting the report required under subsection (d)(3), the Secretary shall participate with program partners in implementing activities to improve snowpack measurement in particular watersheds.

children.label: A
children.text: enhancing activities in river basins to achieve improved snow and water supply forecasting results;

children.label: B
children.text: activities in river basins where snow water supply forecasting related activities described in this section are not occurring on ; andDecember 27, 2020

children.label: C
children.text: demonstrating or testing new, or improving existing, snow and water supply forecasting technology.

children.heading: Information sharing
children.label: 3 Information sharing
children.text: The Secretary may provide information collected and analyzed under this section to program partners through appropriate mechanisms, including interagency agreements with Federal agencies, States, State agencies, or a combination thereof, leases, contracts, cooperative agreements, grants, loans, and memoranda of understanding.

children.heading: Program partners
children.label: 4 Program partners
children.text: Program partners with whom the Secretary enters into cooperative agreements pursuant to paragraph (5) may include water districts, irrigation districts, water associations, universities, State agencies, other Federal agencies, private sector entities, non-governmental organizations, and other entities, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: enter into cooperative agreements with program partners to allow the program to be administered efficiently and cost effectively through cost-sharing or by providing additional in-kind resources necessary for program implementation; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide nonreimbursable matching funding for programmatic and operational activities under this section in consultation with program partners.

children.heading: Environmental laws
children.label: 6 Environmental laws
children.text: Nothing in this section shall modify any obligation of the Secretary to comply with applicable Federal and State environmental laws in carrying out this section.
a list of basins and sub-basins for which snowpack measurement technologies are being used under the program, including a description of each technology used; and
a list of Federal agencies and program partners participating in each basin or sub-basin listed in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $15,000,000, in the aggregate, for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
